name: Multimodal Plant Copilot
type: Voice + chat AI assistant · 3 enterprise clients
short: Voice and chat AI assistants connected to live plant data — answering operational queries, running diagnostics, and surfacing KPIs across three enterprise manufacturing clients. STT, TTS, and LLM reasoning, designed for the floor.
problem: Supervisors and plant managers needed answers on throughput, downtime, and quality without learning a dashboard. The existing path — open a tool, find a filter, screenshot a chart — was too slow for the floor and impossible while their hands were full.
opportunity: voice-first query interface for operators on the floor
natural-language access to live plant KPIs
LLM reasoning over structured + unstructured plant data
one architecture deployable across multiple clients
architecture: Real-time data ingestion → typed query layer
Voice + chat client surfaces · Slack / web / mobile
decisions: Voice and chat run on a single backend — same answers, same audit trail, two front-ends.
STT/TTS stack tuned per client noise environment; no one-size voice pipeline.
Strict scoping of which actions an agent can take versus answer-only mode.
Plant data accessed only through typed tools — never freeform SQL from the LLM.
experience: Live KPI Q&A — “what’s our OEE on line 3 today?”
Diagnostic flows for downtime and quality incidents.
impact: Operational queries answered in seconds, voice or chat.
Led cross-functional team from requirements through deployment — sprint planning, reviews, direct client communication.
Architecture reusable across new plants with client-specific tools.
reflection: A voice agent on a factory floor isn’t a chatbot. It’s a steward of trust between an operator and a system that can’t afford to be wrong.
